outcome_text: Risks associated with transparency and accountability—as identified in the MAP function—are examined and documented.
scoring_text: Skill in evaluating AI transparency and accountability risks, including assessing the adequacy of explainability mechanisms, evaluating accountability structures for AI system decisions, and documenting transparency and accountability risk findings against MAP-phase identified risks.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in evaluating AI transparency and accountability risks, including assessing the adequacy of explainability mechanisms, evaluating accountability structures for AI system decisions, and documenting transparency and accountability risk findings against MAP-phase identified risks.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of responsible AI principles and audit methods, including fairness metric selection, bias test design, explainability documentation standards, and human oversight protocols.
comments: NIST AI RMF MS-2.8 (MEASURE) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-007 is the strongest match (STS: 0.635, cosine: 0.654). Secondary: AG-K-004 (AG, cosine: 0.631), AG-A-002 (AG, cosine: 0.617).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.3/10 (raw 0.635). Shared vocabulary: [ai, explainability]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [accountability, adequacy, against, assessing, decisions, documenting] vs WIDAI terms [audit, bias, design, documentation, fairness, human] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.881 (P=0.875, R=0.886). Balanced P/R — the 33-word competency implication and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.654 ≈ STS 0.635 (gap 0.019). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, explainability] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 11.9%. Shared tokens: [ai, explainability]. NIST AI RMF-only: [accountability, adequacy, against, assessing, decisions, documenting]. WIDAI-only: [audit, bias, design, documentation, fairness, human].
